task_completion.description: Verify the OpenBCI GUI is open and visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI application window is clearly visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify the Synthetic EEG session is running.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI displays active, moving EEG waveforms or graphs, indicating that the synthetic data stream is live.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent triggers the built-in screenshot functionality.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent focuses on the OpenBCI GUI window and inputs the 'm' keystroke, or a visual notification/log message appears within the GUI confirming a screenshot was taken.

task_completion.description: Verify the target Screenshots directory is accessed to show the result.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file explorer or terminal window is open and explicitly shows the path '~/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/'.

task_completion.description: Verify the screenshot file was successfully saved in the correct location.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A newly created image file (e.g., .jpg, .png) is visible inside the '~/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/' directory.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not use external OS-level screenshot utilities.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check terminal commands or GUI actions to ensure tools like 'scrot', 'gnome-screenshot', or OS keyboard shortcuts were NOT used to capture the screen.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not manually move or copy an image to fake the output.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check terminal history or file manager actions to ensure the agent did not use 'mv' or 'cp' commands to place an image into the Screenshots folder.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent interacted directly with the OpenBCI GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should click on or focus the OpenBCI GUI window before pressing the shortcut key, rather than interacting with a different application or terminal to simulate the action.

privileged_info_for_vlm: OpenBCI GUI v5.2.2 features an Expert Mode that enables specific keyboard shortcuts. Pressing the lowercase 'm' key while the GUI is focused automatically takes a screenshot of the application and saves it directly to the '~/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/' directory. The task requires using this built-in feature, not OS-level screenshot tools.
